# ekklesia.gr

> Digital Direct Democracy Platform for Greece. Citizens vote anonymously on real parliamentary bills using Ed25519 cryptography. Non-governmental, open-source (MIT), funded by citizen donations.

## What is ekklesia?

ekklesia.gr (εκκλησία του έθνους) is an independent civic initiative enabling Greek citizens to vote on real bills from the Hellenic Parliament. Votes are cryptographically signed (Ed25519), anonymous, and non-legally binding. The platform calculates a Divergence Score showing how much citizen opinion differs from parliamentary party votes.

## Key Features

- Anonymous voting on real parliamentary bills (Ed25519 signatures)
- Divergence Score: citizen opinion vs. party votes
- MP Comparison: party alignment with citizens
- Local Government: municipal/regional decisions via Diavgeia
- AI Assistant (RAG chatbot) for citizen questions
- Public Forum at pnyx.ekklesia.gr (Discourse)
- Public API (CC BY 4.0) for researchers
- Arweave blockchain archiving for transparency
- Mobile App (Android) + Progressive Web App

## Privacy

- Phone number used ONLY for HLR verification, deleted immediately
- No accounts, no email, no cookies, no tracking
- Private key stored only on user device (Android Keystore / iOS Keychain)
- Server stores only: nullifier hash (non-reversible) + public key + vote signature

## Technology

- Backend: Python FastAPI, PostgreSQL 15, Redis 7
- Frontend: Next.js 14 (SSR, i18n el/en)
- Mobile: Expo React Native
- Crypto: Ed25519 (PyNaCl + @noble/curves)
- AI: Ollama llama3.2 (local) + Claude API (hybrid)
- Forum: Discourse (pnyx.ekklesia.gr)
- Archiving: Arweave permanent storage
- Hosting: Hetzner (Helsinki, EU)

## URLs

- Website: https://ekklesia.gr
- API: https://api.ekklesia.gr/api/v1/
- Forum: https://pnyx.ekklesia.gr
- GitHub: https://github.com/NeaBouli/pnyx
- API Docs: https://ekklesia.gr/wiki/api.html
- Whitepaper: https://ekklesia.gr/wiki/whitepaper.html
